author: William Guy
description: A native study system for your Obsidian vault. LearnKit turns notes into durable knowledge with flashcards, spaced repetition, tests, and more. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: William Guy
manifest.description: A native study system for your vault. LearnKit turns notes into durable knowledge with flashcards, spaced repetition, reviews, tests, and AI tools.
manifest.name: LearnKit

name: LearnKit

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
**Patch release — lint compliance hotfixes only. No functional changes.**

### Hotfixes
- Replaced direct `innerHTML` assignments with sanitized `replaceChildrenWithHTML` across cloze and code-fence rendering paths.
- Switched bare `document` references to `activeDocument` in chart color resolution for popout window compatibility.
- Replaced `instanceof HTMLElement` checks with cross-window-safe `nodeType` comparisons.
- Fixed bare `setTimeout` usage in browser preferences persistence.
- Resolved TypeScript unsafe-type warnings in the docs site content config.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.7.1

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-06-24

### Summary

LearnKit 1.7.0 is a feature release that builds on version 1.6.6. The key changes is update introduces a redesigned sync mode selector which gives your more granular control over where LearnKit can sync and edit flashcards. It also includes several requested bug fixes from the LearnKit community.

### What's Changed

#### **Sync Options**

The first-time sync screen now shows a dropdown with four modes instead of three separate buttons. You can switch anytime in Settings.

- **Simple** (recommended) — Scans only standard LearnKit flashcards. Won't interfere with other plugins that use `::` patterns.
- **Full (Normalize)** — Converts shorthand into LearnKit format. Useful for migrating from other flashcard plugins.
- **Full (Preserve)** — Adds anchor IDs but leaves shorthand as-is. For using LearnKit alongside other flashcard plugins.
- **Off** — Disables sync entirely.

#### **Math Rendering Fixes**
- LaTeX line breaks (`\\`) inside math blocks no longer get collapsed. Matrices, aligned equations, and other multi-line math now display as authored.
- TeX commands like `\begin` in card fields are also handled correctly and won't be mangled when read back from notes.

#### **Code Block Rendering Fixes**
- Backtick code fences in flashcard fields now render reliably as `<pre>` or `<code>` blocks. Cloze deletions inside code blocks display correctly, and the edit modal preserves the closing fence so you never lose it after editing a card.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.7.0

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-05-14

### Summary
LearnKit 1.5.0 is a compatibility release for the Obsidian Community Plugins registry. The focus of this update is making LearnKit install and update cleanly through Obsidian's in-app plugin browser.

### Existing Users
If you're already using LearnKit, back up your current plugin folder before updating. When you install the new community plugin version, copy over everything from your old plugin folder except `main.js`, `styles.css`, and `manifest.json`. Then restore your database from Settings to bring your content back.

If you prefer, you can continue using BRAT instead. That will keep you on the GitHub release track and give you updates as soon as they are published.

### New Users
For a new install, use the Obsidian Community Plugins browser. You can also download the release manually or install through BRAT if you want to follow the GitHub release channel.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.5.0

2026-05-11

### Summary
LearnKit 1.4.1 is a small hotfix release focused on making sync safer and more predictable after 1.4.0.

## Highlights

### Sync Guardrails

### Other Small Fixes

releases.name: LearnKit 1.4.1

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
## Release Date
2026-05-10

## Summary
LearnKit 1.4.0 introduces major study workflow improvements:
- Hotspot and Combo flashcard types
- Expanded internationalization support (including Simplified Chinese translation and additional locale scaffolding)
- Improved shorthand for quick card creation
- Migration support for Obsidian Spaced Repetition users
- UI improvements across library and reading-view modules
- Scheduler, sibling-separation, and image-occlusion improvements and bug fixes

## Full Details
For full technical change detail from the beta cycle and into final 1.4.0 visit [this link](https://github.com/ctrlaltwill/LearnKit/releases/tag/1.4.0-beta).
releases.name: LearnKit 1.4.0

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-05-07

### Summary
LearnKit 1.4.0 Beta is a major release that rolls the unreleased 1.3.4 beta forward alongside other substantial improvements. It introduces Hotspot Cards, Combo Cards, more language options, improved shorthand card syntax, better support for users migrating from Obsidian Spaced Repetition, and several bug fixes.

## Major Features

### Hotspot Cards 
- Turn any image into an interactive identification challenge — ideal for anatomy, geography, art, and more.
- **Two interaction modes:** Click one target at a time, or drag all labels at once. 
- **Region shapes:** using rectangle, circle, freehand or lasso tools, draw the perfect maskffor irregular areas on maps, diagrams or anatomy images.
- [Learn more about Hotspot Cards →](https://ctrlaltwill.github.io/LearnKit/Hotspot-Cards/)

### Combo Cards
- A new subset of basic cards, combo cards allow you to easily define multiple question and answer variants, and LearnKit automatically generates every combination.
- [See examples and full syntax →](https://ctrlaltwill.github.io/LearnKit/Basic-&-Reversed-Flashcards/)
- Combo children appear in sessions and analytics like regular cards. Full lifecycle support in the sync engine.

### Internationalisation
LearnKit now has more language options and we'd love your help adding more.
- **Simplified Chinese (zh-cn)** — full translation by [AKA](https://github.com/lj1446615403-cloud).
- **French, Japanese, Spanish, and Hindi** — scaffolding with basic AI translation available. We'd love review and improvements from community translators.
- **Want to contribute?** See our [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/ctrlaltwill/LearnKit/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) to submit a PR with translations or improvements.

### Expanded Shorthand & Obsidian SR Migration
Card creation syntax is now simpler and more compatible with Obsidian SR users.
- **Basic cards** use `::` — aligned with Obsidian SR conventions.
- **Reversed (bidirectional) cards** use `:::`.
- **Cloze cards** use `cloze::` (or the shorter `cq::`) with `{{c1::hidden}}` markers inline.
- **Auto-migration on sync:** Old `:::` basic cards, Obsidian SR `?`/`??` blocks, and `==text==` inline clozes are automatically converted to LearnKit format.
- New **Sync filter** lets you control which notes become cards by path, tag, or property scope.


## Other Changes

- **Auto-grade for MCQ & Ordered Questions:** Optional settings grade answers immediately — no extra click. Turn off to reveal the answer side first and grade manually.
- **Scheduler fuzz:** Learning and relearning intervals under 2.5 days now include short-term randomness, reducing same-day sibling clustering for new cards.
- **Sibling separation in practice mode:** Disperse and bury rules now apply to practice queues.
- **Freehand & lasso masking** the new mask types from hotspot cards now also work with image occlusion cards.
- **Documentation:** New pages covering updated content, now with demo screenshots for all card types and improved navigation.


## Bug Fixes

- **Bulk edit type toggle:** Toggling a single basic ↔ reversed card in the bulk edit modal (accessed from the Flashcard Libray) now correctly updates the `Q`/`RQ` prefix in markdown. 
- **Bulk edit content truncation:** Editing the last card in a file no longer deletes note content after the card block. 
- **Group editing:** Card group ordering is now normalized on sync, and group editors save correctly from the bulk edit modal.
- **Widget hotkeys:** Fixed an issue where hotkeys did not work for image occlusion cards in the sidebar widget.
- **Inline code & angled brackets:** Backtick-wrapped code and `<angled>` brackets in question text now render correctly when studying.
- **Date-stamped rolling backups:** Daily backups now use dated filenames (e.g. `daily-backup-20260430.db`) instead of overwriting a single file, preserving the previous day's snapshot.
- **Testing:** 17 new unit tests added. Full suite now at 922 tests.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.4.0 Beta

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-04-28

### Summary
LearnKit 1.3.4 introduces Hotspot Cards, a new interactive card type for visual identification and spatial learning.

## What's New
- **Hotspot Cards** — Study spatial relationships through image-based challenges.
- **Two interaction modes** — Drag-and-drop labels onto the image, or use point-and-click to mark locations.
- **Visual-first learning workflows** — Designed for subjects where spatial awareness matters, including gross anatomy, histology, radiology, geography, astronomy, art, and engineering.
- **Advanced Image Occlusion tools** — Freeform and Smart mask types introduced for Hotspot Cards are now available in the image occlusion editor for broader use.

## What's Changed
- Hotspot review feedback now keeps anchors fixed for click mode and adapts label placement around markers for better readability.
- Drag/drop hotspot behavior now respects the **Show drop location hint** setting on front-side rendering and placement behavior.
- Session hotspot label ordering on reveal now places the answer label before the image block.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.3.4 Beta

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-04-26

### Summary
LearnKit 1.3.3 is a hotfix release focused primarily on fixing a mobile plugin load bug. The mobile version of the plugin should now launch successfully again.

## Key Fixes
- Fixed a mobile plugin load failure.

## What's Changed
- Improved sibling card study flow and updated documentation.
- Improved nested cloze parsing.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.3.3

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-04-24


### Summary
LearnKit 1.3.2 makes Companion feel smoother and introduces cloze hints to make recall prompts clearer without giving away the full answer.

## What's New
- **Cloze hints** — you can now write clozes with hints `{{c1::Psoriatic arthritis::diagnosis}}`. While the card is hidden, LearnKit shows `diagnosis` as the cue instead of a blank. In typed cloze mode, that same hint appears as the input placeholder. When you reveal the card, LearnKit will show the full answer: `Psoriatic arthritis`.
- **Better streaming controls in Companion** — if a reply is still streaming, you can stop it  instead of waiting for the full response.

## What's Changed
- Companion is better at understanding short follow-up requests, so messages like "do it" or "add it to my note" behave more predictably.
- Companion edit suggestions are easier to review, with clearer inline change previews and a smoother accept or reject flow.
- Quick Cards now supports cloze hints too, so shorthand like `cloze:::The capital of {{France::country}} is {{Paris::city}}` expands correctly.
- **Organising groups is now easier** – sync now keeps your flashcard group rows in alphabetical order, and saving an edited card now resyncs that note immediately so comma-separated `G | ... |` lists stay alphabetised without a manual sync.
- **Parent/child groups behave consistently** – LearnKit now treats `Parent/Child` and `Parent::Child` as the same hierarchy in edit fields and study scopes, so child groups stay nested correctly without collapsing separate groups into one path.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.3.2

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-04-21


### Summary
LearnKit 1.3.1 is a small maintenance release that makes Companion more reliable and smooths out the overall app experience.

## What's New
- Improved the Companion AI compatibility workflow so model support is easier to track and validate.
- Expanded the supporting guidance so setup, testing and troubleshooting are easier to follow.

## What's Changed
- Tidied up release and documentation work so updates are easier to manage.
- Smoothed out the look and feel across key study screens for a more consistent experience.

## Fixes
- Fixed a set of small interaction and styling issues that could make controls feel inconsistent.
- Smoothed out settings navigation and other minor interface regressions.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.3.1

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-04-18 

### Summary
LearnKit 1.3.0 introduces agentic Companion features, external TTS providers, Quick Cards shorthand, streaming AI replies and more.

## What's New
- **Agentic Companion** — Companion can now edit, create, and refactor your notes with inline diff previews and accept/reject controls. All edits require user approval before being added to your notes.
- **External TTS providers** — use OpenAI, ElevenLabs, Google Cloud, or a custom HTTP endpoint for text-to-speech instead of the built-in system voice. Includes voice and model selection, per-provider API key storage, and a local audio cache to avoid repeat API calls.
- **TTS support for more card-types** — TTS now reads MCQ cards with numbered options on the front and announces the correct answer on the back. 
- **Quick Cards** — Making basic and cloze cards has got even easier! Simply write `Question:::Answer` anywhere in a note and LearnKit expands it to the full card format on sync. Or for clozes use this shorthand: `cloze:::The capital of {{France}} is {{Paris}}` which auto-numbers tokens and expands to the correct LearnKit format.
- **Streaming Companion replies** — AI chat and review replies now stream token-by-token for a more responsive feel and allowing you to see responses sooner!
- **Inline test generation and grading** — Companion can generate a practice test in chat and grade your answers inline, with per-question feedback and scoring.

## What's Changed
- Grading, undo, bury, and suspend actions are now handled by dedicated service modules, ensuring consistent behaviour across the reviewer, widget, and gatekeeper surfaces.
- Reintroduced rotating greeting messages on the home view.
- Updated deck and hierarchy organisation documentation and in-app guides. Frontmatter tagging for decks is on the roadmap for a future release.
- I18n strings are ready for community translation.

## Bug Fixes
- Fixed image occlusion children not updating when group assignments are changed on edit.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.3.0

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:

### Summary
LearnKit 1.3.0 introduces agentic Companion features, external TTS providers, Quick Cards shorthand, streaming AI replies and more.

## What's New

- **Quick Cards** — Making basic and cloze cards has got even easier! Simply write `Question:::Answer` anywhere in a note and LearnKit expands it to the full card format on sync. Or for clozes use this shorthand: `cloze:::The capital of {{France}} is {{Paris}}` which auto-numbers tokens and expands to the correct LearnKit format.

- **Inline test generation and grading** — Companion can generate a practice test in chat and grade your answers inline, with per-question feedback and scoring.

## What's Changed

releases.name: LearnKit 1.3.0 Beta

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-04-12

### Summary
This release has big UI updates across many views and rebuilds internationalisation to make it ready for community translation. There are also bug fixes and tweaks to components like Tests (exam generator). Read the full notes below to find out more! 

## What's Changed

### UI & Styling
- Broad CSS refinements across analytics, browser, buttons, cards, deck rows, dropdowns, exam generator, header, home, layout, modals, note review, pretty cards, session, settings, study assistant, tooltips, and widget views.
- Removed expanding button labels and aligned assistant header states.

### Internationalisation
- Introduced a base + override locale architecture (`en-base.json` as the primary nonical English source, with `en-gb.json` and `en-us.json` providing spelling overrides only).
- Added a token alias map (`token-aliases.json`) enabling old or duplicate locale keys to resolve without breaking existing references.
- Added `tPlural()` and `pluralSuffix()` translation helpers for count-aware strings.
- Cleared all remaining hardcoded UI literals across 20+ source files, wrapping them with `t()` / `_tx()`.
- Moved non-translatable decorators (ellipsis, arrows, emoji, bullet separators, symbols) out of locale JSON and into TypeScript call sites so translators only see real text. Did similar for leading/trailing whitespaces.
- Updated `CONTRIBUTING.md` with the translation workflow and locale file conventions.

### Exam Generator
- Added support for multi-select multiple-choice questions in test generation.
- Fixed an issue where test answers longer than the viewport could not be scrolled ([#118](https://github.com/ctrlaltwill/LearnKit/issues/118)).
- Updated AI grading prompts for more accurate and consistent scoring.
- Normalised exam generator UI layout and styling.

### Scheduler
- Fixed bury duration: cards are now buried for 24 hours instead of until UTC midnight, preventing same-day re-appearance in certain time zones.

### Reading View
- Fixed LaTeX rendering in reading view ([#125](https://github.com/ctrlaltwill/LearnKit/issues/125)).

### Home & Navigation
- Fixed deck navigation from the home screen when selecting pinned or recent decks.
- Hidden the "practice" cue for scopes with no cards.

### Internal
- Inlined the `MixinConstructor` type alias, removing the ambient declaration file.
- Resolved CI lint failures introduced during the i18n commit.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.2.9

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-04-05

### Summary
LearnKit 1.2.8 is a maintenance release focused on post-migration bug fixes and stability improvements.

## What's Changed
- Fixed styling regressions introduced after the migration so affected UI elements render correctly again.
- Fixed a cloze deletion bug related to child element handling in reviewer questions.
- Resolved TypeScript errors to restore clean type-checking and improve reliability.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.2.8

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-04-02

### Summary
LearnKit 1.2.7 is a maintenance release completing the LearnKit rebranding, fixing bugs and updating project dependencies.

## What's Changed
- Completed LearnKit rename across codebase.
- Fixed scope filter tokens with spaces not being URI-encoded.
- Upgraded undo to a 3-frame stack.
- Migrated docs site from VitePress to Astro Starlight.
- Fixed timer leak in widget/reviewer auto-advance.
- Improved keyboard focus restoration after session transitions.
- Added response validation for external API calls.
- Added unit tests for view-layer pure logic and SQLite scheduling modules.
- Added migration tests.
- Replaced prototype monkey-patching with a TypeScript mixin chain.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.2.7

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-03-29

### Summary
LearnKit 1.2.6 introduces FSRS optimisation, deck browser improvements and bug fixes.

## What's New
- FSRS optimisation has been added. You can now optimise FSRS parameters based on your own review history.

## What's Changed
- Fixed issues where some settings were not saving reliably.
- Updated the deck browser column definitions to make counts easier to understand:
	- `Due`: Review cards ready right now.
	- `New`: Cards never studied (all new cards in the deck).
	- `Learn`: Learning and relearning cards ready right now.
	- `Total`: Every card in the deck.
- Added a new button to hide the title bar while studying flashcards.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.2.6

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-03-27

### Summary
LearnKit 1.2.5 is a new update focused on enhanced mobile support, clearer settings, and more options for adding context to AI requests.

## What's Changed
- Better support for the Obsidian mobile app with improved layout and interactions.
- Settings labels and descriptions have been updated for better clarity.
- Updated settings for attachments and context behavior with AI tools.
- Ongoing UI refinements across all aspects of the plugin.

## What's New
- Improved default context-source behavior in Study Assistant generation.
- New scheduling option to enable interval fuzzing (on by default) for better due-date spread.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.2.5

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-03-19

### Summary
LearnKit 1.2.4 is a hotfix release focused on compatibility and quality fixes for issues.

## What's Changed
- Option for two/four button grading has been unhidden from settings menu.
- Fix for Companion hitbox; it now uses mouseenter and mouseleave behavior while allowing clicks on note content behind the button.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.2.4

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-03-23

### Summary
LearnKit 1.2.3 is a major overhaul release focused on app-wide UI improvements, plus significant updates to Coach and Tests functionality.

## What's New

### Massive UI Overhaul
- Massive UI and UX fixes across the entire app for more consistent layouts, spacing, and interactions.
- Improved visual stability across major views and modals, including fewer overlap and rendering issues.
- Refined controls, menus, and workflow transitions for a cleaner and faster day-to-day experience.
- Improved responsiveness across desktop and mobile-sized layouts.

### Coach Updates
- Updated Coach workflows for clearer guided practice and smoother prompt-to-response flow.
- Improved Coach session reliability and consistency during longer study runs.
- Better integration of Coach within the broader study workflow.

### Tests Updates
- Major Tests UX improvements for clearer rounds, better readability, and smoother progression.
- Improved test generation and run flow so setup is faster and more consistent.
- Better handling for larger test sessions and repeat practice workflows.

## Important: Plugin ID Migration

This release includes a plugin ID update. Please follow the migration steps below to preserve your existing data.

### Steps To Migrate (Recommended)
1. Open **Settings -> Storage and Sync** and create a manual backup.
2. Confirm the backup completed, then fully close Obsidian.
3. In your vault, rename `.obsidian/plugins/sprout/` to `.obsidian/plugins/learnkit/`.
4. Copy the new release files (`main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`) from your local `dist` output into `.obsidian/plugins/learnkit/`.
5. Reopen Obsidian and re-enable LearnKit if needed.
6. Return to **Storage and Sync** and restore from the backup.
7. Confirm your data is restored, then continue using LearnKit as normal.

### If You Already Installed The Update
1. Open your plugin folders and copy any existing data from `.obsidian/plugins/sprout/` into `.obsidian/plugins/learnkit/`.
2. Copy these items if present: `backups`, `chats`, `configuration`, `scheduling`, and `data.json`.
3. Keep the new release files in `learnkit` (`main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`) from the latest version.
4. Reopen Obsidian and re-enable LearnKit if needed.
5. Go to **Storage and Sync** in LearnKit settings and restore from your latest backup.
6. Confirm your data is restored, then continue using LearnKit as normal.

## Notes
- This is a high-impact release that touches many screens and workflows.
- If you notice issues or bugs, please raise them so we can prioritize follow-up fixes quickly.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.2.3

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-03-19

### Summary
LearnKit 1.2.2 is a hotfix release focused on compatibility and quality fixes for issues.

### What's New
- Companion now supports attachments. You can attach files directly to the chat from the vault or from local files, and there is also a setting to allow Companion to access attachments in the open note (for example attached images, PowerPoint slides, PDFs, and Word documents). These attachments can be used to provide context, for example for generating flashcards.
- Tests now support attachments. You can attach files directly when generating tests, and there is also a setting to allow Companion to access attachments in notes. This means if you choose Note A as the test scope and this note contains Attachment B, this will also be included in the context for generating the test.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.2.2

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-03-18

### Summary
LearnKit 1.2.1 is a hotfix release focused on compatibility and quality fixes. This update includes changes to meet Obsidian community plugin lint requirements and related cleanup improvements.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.2.1

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-03-18

### Summary
LearnKit 1.2.0 is a major overhaul release with three new features: Tests, Coach, and Note Review.

## What's New

### Tests
- Added Tests for auto-generating study tests from your notes and learning context.
- Build structured question rounds quickly and identify weak areas for follow-up study.

### Coach
- Added Coach workflows for turning note content into guided prompts and focused practice.
- Coach helps bridge passive reading and active recall.

### Note Review
- Added Note Review workflows to support pass/fail review loops on notes.
- Note Review helps bring your notes into the same spaced repetition loop as your cards.

## Notes for Early Users
- LearnKit is still in its infancy and there will be bugs and rough edges.
- We deeply appreciate all early users and everyone who reports issues or shares feedback.

## Open Source Mission
- LearnKit is being built as an open source study plugin for Obsidian.
- The goal is to expand Obsidian learning functionality and move toward the depth of premium subscription tools (for example, RemNote-style workflows) while keeping data local and user-controlled.
releases.name: LearnKit 1.2.0

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-03-10

### Summary
Sprout 1.1.0 is a major update introducing Sprig, a built-in AI learning assistant with bring-your-own-key support, plus reliability and workflow improvements across reading, editing, and studying.

## What's New

### Sprig - AI Assistant
- Bring-your-own API key support so you can connect your preferred provider and model.
- Ask and review modes for note-aware study assistance.
- Generate mode to create flashcards from note context and insert directly into your notes.

## What's Improved

### Reading and Editing Experience
- Markdown-style text area editing, allowing you to incorporate lists and other markdown features easily into your cards.

### Data and Platform
- Continued rollout of SQLite-backed storage improvements for reliability and scale.
- Image occlusion improvements, including OCR and auto-masking.

## Notes
- Sprig uses your own provider key; pricing depends on your selected provider/model.
- For setup details, use the in-app assistant settings and guide content.


## What's Coming

### Sync Efficiency
- Future updates will focus on more efficient syncing, reducing full-database updates each session for users who study across devices with cloud-synced vaults.

### Mobile Functionality
- A major focus going forward is improving the Sprout experience on mobile and tablet, where portability is especially important for many learners.

### Reading View
- We also want to give you full control over how cards look in Reading View. More work is planned for visual styles, custom layouts, and CSS options so you can present flashcards exactly how you want.
releases.name: Sprout 1.1.0

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-03-05

### Summary
Sprout 1.0.9 adds new study features, better backups, better support for LaTeX, more security settings, and updated docs and translation support.

## What's Changed

### Added
- Added reminders and gatekeeper popups to remind you when cards are due and encourage regular review.
- Added translation framework support to prepare for future crowd-sourced translations.
- Added flag tokens (see below).

### Flags
- Added inline language flag tokens in card text as visual hints for language learning (for example `{{es}}` and `{{gb}}`).
- Example: `Q | {{es}} Hola |` and `A | {{gb}} Hello |` shows a clear visual cue for each language and helps learners switch context faster.
- Added flag-aware audio routing: in **Settings → Audio → Flag-aware routing**, the same flags can route TTS to a matching language/accent voice (with optional spoken language labels). This improves TTS voice detection.

### Changed
- Updated colour palette for grading buttons.
- Improved LaTeX rendering consistency across Study, Widget, and Reading views.
- Added support for clozes inside LaTeX content within questions.
- Improved data backup reliability and recovery behaviour.
- Added options to control how your card data is backed up.

### Fixed
- Improved clarity of revealed answers for ordered questions.

### Documentation
- New wiki pages for added features, plus updated pages for modified content.
- Updated the documentation website to utilise VitePress for online users.
releases.name: Sprout 1.0.9

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-03-01

### Summary
Hotfix release focused on reliability and widget UX improvements after 1.0.7.

## What's Changed

### Code Fixes
- Adjusted z-index layering for study buttons and modals to prevent conflicts

### Widget Updates
- Fixed widget location and launch behavior: widget no longer auto-opens on app reload and is now launched from the command palette
- Updated widget colors for better readability in non-translucent windows

### New Features
- Added transfer of active study context/session from the widget to the main Sprout tab
- Introduced updated iconography and new branding assets

releases.name: Sprout 1.0.6

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date
2026-02-18

### Summary
Version 1.0.5 is a major feature release introducing new card types, text-to-speech, a new settings view and greater customisation of reading-view and Sprout card styles. Experimental support for mobile functionality has also been added. 

## What's Changed

### Cards and customisation
- Multi-select MCQ: multiple correct answers selectable per question
- Ordered questions: memorise sequences by arranging items in the correct order
- Typed cloze: type the answer directly into cloze deletion cards
- Image occlusion mask customisation: adjust mask appearance and styling

### Text-to-Speech
- Added support for text-to-speech in basic and cloze card types
- Utilises native voices and allows customisation based on alphabet used
- Additional settings for voice-tuning 

### Reading view
- Overhaul with two distinct styles: Flashcards and Clean markdown
- Additional styles and full Custom Reading style support planned for a future update

### Settings and guide
- New settings view with expanded controls and clearer organisation
- Updated in-app guide surfaced directly within Obsidian

## In development
- Mobile functionality support
- Continued bug-fix and stability updates
- Reading view enhancements and additional style support
- Codebase tidy-up focused on extensibility

## Note
If scheduling or analytics data appears missing after update, restore from backup via Settings → Storage and Backup.
releases.name: Sprout 1.0.5

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
### Release Date

February 8, 2026

### Summary

Version 1.0.4 includes a major dependency upgrade to Recharts v3, incorporating three Dependabot pull requests, along with minor styling improvements.

## What's Changed

### Dependency Updates

- Merged #1: Bump globals from 15.15.0 to latest
- Merged #2: Bump recharts from 2.15.4 to 3.7.0
- Merged #3: Bump @types/node from 20.x to latest
### Recharts Fixes

- Updated from Recharts 2.15.4 to 3.7.0
- Fixed compatibility issues in analytics charts (forgetting curve, review calendar heatmap)
- Resolved type definitions for chart components
- Ensured all analytics visualizations work with new API


### Minor Fixes

- Tag box styling consistency across browser, modals, and card editor
- Applied uniform background, border, and shadow styling to all tag boxes
releases.name: Sprout 1.0.4

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
# Release Notes – Version 1.0.3

### Release Date
February 8, 2026

### Summary
Version 1.0.3 is a quality-of-life release focused on settings reorganization, backup improvements, and modal consistency.

## What's Changed

### Settings Overhaul
- Reorganized settings into clearer sections: General, Study, Scheduling, and Storage
- Improved labels and descriptions for all settings
- Automatic migration of existing settings on first load

### Backup System
- Scheduling-data-only backups (smaller file sizes)
- Automatic backup interval (15 minutes)
- Automatic retention limit (keeps 5 most recent backups)

### Modal Styling
- Consistent overlay across all modals (Add Card, Edit Flashcard, Bulk Edit, Quick Edit)
- Unified layout, header, and button styling
- Proper DOM cleanup when closing modals

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed type issues in forgetting-curve chart and review-calendar heatmap
- Fixed import statements in browser-helpers and title-markdown
- Removed unused variables and dead CSS classes
releases.name: Sprout 1.0.3

releases.author: ctrlaltwill
releases.description:
# Release Notes – Version 1.0.0

### Release Date
February 5, 2026

### Summary
Sprout 1.0.0 is the first stable release — an Obsidian plugin for Markdown-first flashcards with FSRS scheduling, analytics, and flexible card formats.

## What's Changed

### Core Features
- FSRS-based spaced repetition scheduler
- Multiple card types: basic, cloze, multiple choice, and image occlusion
- Analytics dashboard with charts and heatmaps
- Card browser with search, filter, and bulk edit tools
- Inline editor for creating and editing cards directly in notes
- Reading view cards for excerpts and highlights
- Markdown-first workflow with note-linked cards
releases.name: Sprout 1.0.0
